llm_reasoning: The location's extremely low mean annual temperature (2.3°C) and high frost risk (26.7%) are severe limiting factors, making it highly unsuitable for maize, a warm-season crop. While soil organic carbon (22.85 g/kg) and clay content (35.8%) are positive for fertility and water retention, they cannot compensate for the adverse climate. Operational risks include complete crop failure due to insufficient heat units and severe frost damage. Mitigation involves selecting ultra-early, cold-tolerant varieties, but alternative, cold-hardy crops are strongly recommended.
